vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of critical collapse. The registry (P1-P4) is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), and P4 (Silver Guest) are absent or indistinguishable from bare, dark soil. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable specimen, though it is severely compromised.

plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The specimen shows a single, thin, chlorotic stem with minimal leaf mass. The white rabbit anchor is present but the plant itself is failing to thrive, likely due to insufficient light or substrate exhaustion.
plant_audit.Soil_Condition: All pots contain dark, damp-appearing soil with no visible signs of healthy root systems or secondary growth.

biome_observations.Biome_Anomalies: Presence of white, crystalline debris (likely crushed eggshells or mineral deposits) in the P3 pot. No fungal blooms observed, but the lack of plant matter suggests a sterile or hostile environment.

temporal_deltas.Earliest_to_T-5: Initial state shows a single green stem in P3. Other pots are empty.
temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Progressive loss of turgor in the P3 stem. The stem appears to be leaning or collapsing further toward the soil surface.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: Stasis in a state of decay. No recovery or new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is experiencing a total failure of the expected botanical population. The Pothos (P3) is in a terminal decline phase. The absence of P1, P2, and P4 indicates either total decomposition or removal from the pots.
vision_report.anomalies: The white crystalline fragments in P3 are a clear 'New Intervention'. The lack of any foliage in the yellow pot (P1) and the black pot (P2/P4) confirms a systemic loss of the registered specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a desolate botanical environment. The only remaining specimen, a Pothos in the P3 pot, is struggling to survive in a low-light, likely nutrient-poor environment. The other pots are devoid of life, suggesting a failure of the initial planting or a catastrophic event leading to the loss of the other three species. The presence of white debris suggests an attempt at soil amendment that has failed to yield results.
